perturbation.original_text: The term “ Representatives ” in the case of JDA shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, investment bankers, financial advisors, agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of JDA. The term “ Representatives ” in the case of RHI shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, and with prior written consent of JDA (not to be unreasonably withheld), investment bankers, financial advisors, potential sources of capital or financing (debt or equity), agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of RHI. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing in this Agreement shall restrict the ability of RHI to discuss with, or engage, Greenhill & Co, or Bain & Company to act as its consultant, investment banker or financial advisor in connection with a Possible Transaction or to discuss with, or engage, Deloitte as its advisor or accountant in connection with a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Representatives ” in the case of JDA shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, investment bankers, financial advisors, agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of JDA. The term “ Representatives ” in the case of RHI shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of RHI. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing in this Agreement shall restrict the ability of RHI to discuss with, or engage, Greenhill & Co, or Bain & Company to act as its consultant in connection with a Possible Transaction or to discuss with, or engage, Deloitte as its advisor or accountant in connection with a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "financial advisor, potential sources of capital or financing (debt or equity)" and "investment banker or financial advisor" in the definition of "Representatives" for RHI, it creates an in-text contradiction because RHI's ability to engage financial advisors and potential capital sources is restricted without prior JDA written consent, which makes the exceptions for Greenhill & Co, Bain & Company not clear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) The term “ Representatives ” in the case of JDA shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, investment bankers, financial advisors, agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of JDA. The term “ Representatives ” in the case of RHI shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of RHI. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing in this Agreement shall restrict the ability of RHI to discuss with, or engage, Greenhill & Co, or Bain & Company to act as its consultant in connection with a Possible Transaction or to discuss with, or engage, Deloitte as its advisor or accountant in connection with a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed RHI to engage Greenhill & Co, Bain & Company as financial advisors/investment bankers, and Deloitte as its advisor/accountant without JDA's consent, despite the general requirement for consent for other representatives. The changed text removes "financial advisor, potential sources of capital or financing (debt or equity)" from the definition of RHI's 'Representatives' and "investment banker or financial advisor" from the exception clause for Greenhill & Co, Bain & Company, but the contradicted text (which mirrors the changed text) doesn't fully remove the concept of financial advisors/investment bankers for RHI. This removal restricts RHI's ability to engage financial advisors and potential capital sources without JDA's prior written consent, creating a contradiction with the clause that seemingly allows them to engage specific firms (Greenhill & Co and Bain & Company) for consultancy without such consent. This falls under Omissions - In Text Contradiction as crucial language was removed creating a conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to Section 5, each Party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other Party, such Party and its Representatives will not disclose to any other Person (i) that Evaluation Material has been exchanged between the Parties, (ii) that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Parties concerning a Possible Transaction or (iii) any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof); provided , however , that JDA may make such disclosure if it does not identify RHI by name or by identifiable description. RHI agrees that neither RHI nor any Representative (to the extent acting on behalf or at the direction of RHI) of RHI will, without the prior consent of JDA (not to be unreasonably withheld), directly or indirectly, enter into any agreement, arrangement or understanding with any other person regarding a Possible Transaction (including, without limitation, financing thereof). RHI represents and warrants that, except as disclosed to JDA or its outside counsel prior to the date hereof, neither RHI nor any Representative of RHI have, prior to the date hereof, taken any of the actions referred to in the immediately preceding sentence. Without limiting the foregoing, RHI agrees that neither RHI nor any Representative (to the extent acting on behalf and at the direction of RHI) of RHI will, without the prior written consent of JDA, enter into any exclusive arrangement with respect to the provision of debt financing in connection with a Possible Transaction. For purposes of this Agreement, any agreement, arrangement or other understanding, whether written or oral, with any potential debt financing source which does, or could be reasonably expected to, legally or contractually limit, restrict or otherwise impair in any manner, directly or indirectly, such financing source from acting as a potential debt financing source to any other party with respect to a Potential Transaction shall be deemed an exclusive arrangement.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to Section 5, each Party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other Party, such Party and its Representatives will not disclose to any other Person (i) that Evaluation Material has been exchanged between the Parties, (ii) that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Parties concerning a Possible Transaction or (iii) any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof); provided , however , that JDA may make such disclosure if it does not identify RHI by name or by identifiable description. RHI agrees that neither RHI nor any Representative (to the extent acting on behalf or at the direction of RHI) of RHI will, without the prior consent of JDA (not to be unreasonably withheld), directly or indirectly, enter into any agreement, arrangement or understanding with any other person regarding a Possible Transaction. RHI represents and warrants that, except as disclosed to JDA or its outside counsel prior to the date hereof, neither RHI nor any Representative of RHI have, prior to the date hereof, taken any of the actions referred to in the immediately preceding sentence. Without limiting the foregoing, RHI agrees that neither RHI nor any Representative (to the extent acting on behalf and at the direction of RHI) of RHI will, without the prior written consent of JDA, enter into any exclusive arrangement with respect to the provision of debt financing in connection with a Possible Transaction. For purposes of this Agreement, any agreement, arrangement or other understanding, whether written or oral, with any potential debt financing source which does, or could be reasonably expected to, legally or contractually limit, restrict or otherwise impair in any manner, directly or indirectly, such financing source from acting as a potential debt financing source to any other party with respect to a Potential Transaction shall be deemed an exclusive arrangement.
perturbation.explanation: Removing "(including, without limitation, financing thereof)" from the sentence where RHI needs prior consent creates an in-text contradiction. Without it, it's unclear if the subsequent sentence about "exclusive arrangement with respect to the provision of debt financing" is still covered by the general prohibition on entering agreements without JDA's consent. The contract now implies that RHI can enter agreements regarding a Possible Transaction *except* for exclusive debt financing, but that's not explicitly stated, causing ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Non-Disclosure of Discussions . Subject to Section 5, each Party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other Party, such Party and its Representatives will not disclose to any other Person (i) that Evaluation Material has been exchanged between the Parties, (ii) that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Parties concerning a Possible Transaction or (iii) any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof); provided , however , that JDA may make such disclosure if it does not identify RHI by name or by identifiable description. RHI agrees that neither RHI nor any Representative (to the extent acting on behalf or at the direction of RHI) of RHI will, without the prior consent of JDA (not to be unreasonably withheld), directly or indirectly, enter into any agreement, arrangement or understanding with any other person regarding a Possible Transaction. RHI represents and warrants that, except as disclosed to JDA or its outside counsel prior to the date hereof, neither RHI nor any Representative of RHI have, prior to the date hereof, taken any of the actions referred to in the immediately preceding sentence. Without limiting the foregoing, RHI agrees that neither RHI nor any Representative (to the extent acting on behalf and at the direction of RHI) of RHI will, without the prior written consent of JDA, enter into any exclusive arrangement with respect to the provision of debt financing in connection with a Possible Transaction. For purposes of this Agreement, any agreement, arrangement or other understanding, whether written or oral, with any potential debt financing source which does, or could be reasonably expected to, legally or contractually limit, restrict or otherwise impair in any manner, directly or indirectly, such financing source from acting as a potential debt financing source to any other party with respect to a Potential Transaction shall be deemed an exclusive arrangement.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes "(including, without limitation, financing thereof)" which means that agreements regarding a Possible Transaction includes financing. The Changed Text removes this phrase. Therefore, the Changed Text creates ambiguity about whether the general prohibition on entering agreements without JDA's consent covers exclusive debt financing, potentially contradicting the earlier prohibition and causing a contradiction to the contradicted text because, as it stands in the Changed Text, it is unclear if the sentence about 'exclusive arrangement with respect to the provision of debt financing' is still covered by the general prohibition. As such, this is an Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient and its Representatives may disclose such information, and need not provide such notice, in connection with a routine blanket audit or proceeding (including in response to oral questions or requests for information or documents) involving the Recipient or its Representatives, as applicable, by a regulatory authority with jurisdiction over the Recipient or such Representative where neither the Provider nor the Possible Transaction is the target of such proceeding or audit. In either case, the Recipient and its Representatives shall take reasonable measures to obtain confidential treatment with respect to any such information disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient and its Representatives may disclose such information, and need not provide such notice, in connection with a routine blanket audit or proceeding (including in response to oral questions or requests for information or documents) involving the Recipient or its Representatives, as applicable, by a regulatory authority with jurisdiction over the Recipient or such Representative where neither the Provider nor the Possible Transaction is the target of such proceeding or audit.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence "In either case, the Recipient and its Representatives shall take reasonable measures to obtain confidential treatment with respect to any such information disclosed.", the contract introduces uncertainty regarding the obligations of the Recipient and its Representatives in such an event. It still says that the Recipient may disclose, but without the last sentence that they shall take reasonable measures, create an in-text contradiction of what is expected of them, especially when dealing with confidential Evaluation Material
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Legally Required Disclosure . If the Recipient or its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, other requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited 2 under Section 4 above, the Recipient or such Representative shall provide the Provider with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement. If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Provider, the Recipient or any of its Representatives is nonetheless legally compelled or required by law to disclose Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4, the Recipient or its Representatives may, without liability hereunder, disclose to such requiring Person only that portion of such Evaluation Material or any such facts which the Recipient or its Representatives is legally compelled or required by law to disclose; provided that the Recipient and/or its Representatives exercise its commercially reasonable efforts, at the Provider’s sole expense, to preserve the confidentiality of such Evaluation Material or any of such facts, including, without limitation, by reasonably cooperating with the Provider to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Material or such facts by the Person receiving the material. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient and its Representatives may disclose such information, and need not provide such notice, in connection with a routine blanket audit or proceeding (including in response to oral questions or requests for information or documents) involving the Recipient or its Representatives, as applicable, by a regulatory authority with jurisdiction over the Recipient or such Representative where neither the Provider nor the Possible Transaction is the target of such proceeding or audit.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit requirement for the Recipient and its Representatives to take reasonable measures to obtain confidential treatment. The contradicted text outlines scenarios where disclosure is legally required, and it also originally had a clause that dictated how the recipient should behave during a routine audit and what measure to take. By removing the sentence about taking reasonable measures, it creates a contradiction. The first portion of the contradicted text requires commercially reasonable efforts to maintain confidentiality, while the changed text creates an exception but removes the part about reasonable measures. Since the omission removes a key obligation relating to confidentiality, it creates a direct conflict and contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with the previous sentence. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the first sentence introduces uncertainty and a potential in-text contradiction. While the section states the Recipient must return or destroy Evaluation Material, removing the certificate of compliance means there is no formal method for the Provider to verify that the Recipient followed through. This weakens the enforceability of the destruction/return clause, as there's no required proof. Without the first sentence, it's not clear how the Provider will know if the Recipient is still bound.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Termination of Discussions . If either Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly inform the other Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the written request of the Provider for any reason, the Recipient will, and will direct its Representatives to, within five business days after receipt of such notice or request, destroy or return all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained (except that one copy may be maintained by outside legal counsel to the Recipient for archival purposes), and the term of this Agreement shall be extended by a like number of days for each day that the Recipient or any of its Representatives is in non-compliance of this Section 8. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the Recipient to provide a certificate of compliance to the Provider, ensuring verification of the return or destruction of Evaluation Material. The changed text removes this requirement. The Contradicted Text (Section 8) discusses the return or destruction of Evaluation Material. By removing the certificate of compliance requirement, the Changed Text creates uncertainty about whether the Recipient has actually complied with Section 8, weakening its enforceability and thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as it removes a verification step that was previously in place.

perturbation.original_text: RHI also agrees during the Standstill Period not to request (either directly or through its affiliates or Representatives) that JDA (or its directors, officers, employees or agents), directly or indirectly, amend or waive any provision of this Section 10 (including this sentence) if such request would require JDA to publicly disclose such request. In no event shall this Section 10 be construed as prohibiting the taking of any of the aforementioned actions with respect to any Person other than JDA or any of its subsidiaries notwithstanding the fact that, at the time such action is taken, JDA (or a subsidiary thereof) may be a subsidiary of such Person.
perturbation.changed_text: In no event shall this Section 10 be construed as prohibiting the taking of any of the aforementioned actions with respect to any Person other than JDA or any of its subsidiaries notwithstanding the fact that, at the time such action is taken, JDA (or a subsidiary thereof) may be a subsidiary of such Person.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the first sentence about RHI not requesting amendments or waivers that would require public disclosure, it creates a contradiction. The standstill is supposed to prevent RHI from taking certain actions, but by omitting the restriction on requesting amendments, RHI could now indirectly pressure JDA to publicly disclose information related to the potential transaction. While RHI cannot directly act, they can request JDA to publicly disclose, which goes against the standstill purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Standstill . RHI agrees that, for a period ending on the earlier of (x) one year after the date of this Agreement, (y) the date a public announcement is made of the entry by JDA into a binding definitive agreement with any third party to effect a purchase, tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination that, if consummated, would result in a third party owning at least a majority of the outstanding voting securities of JDA or all or substantially all of the assets of JDA and its subsidiaries (taken as a whole) or (z) the date of commencement by a third party of a tender or exchange offer for at least a majority of the outstanding voting securities of JDA (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by JDA or its Representatives, neither RHI nor any of its affiliates (as such term is defined under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ 1934 Act ”)) which has been furnished with Evaluation Material pursuant hereto (including New Mountain Capital LLC) or Representatives (acting on its behalf) will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of the equity securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or any material assets of JDA or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving JDA or any of its subsidiaries, 4 (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to JDA or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of JDA; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the 1934 Act with respect to the voting securities of JDA; (c) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit an unsolicited proposal for or offer of (with or without condition), any extraordinary transaction involving JDA or its voting securities or assets; (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to change, control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of JDA; (e) take any action which might force JDA to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; (f) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing; or (g) make any public announcement inconsistent with the agreements contained in this Section, In no event shall this Section 10 be construed as prohibiting the taking of any of the aforementioned actions with respect to any Person other than JDA or any of its subsidiaries notwithstanding the fact that, at the time such action is taken, JDA (or a subsidiary thereof) may be a subsidiary of such Person.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prevented RHI from requesting amendments or waivers that would compel JDA to publicly disclose information, which aligns with the standstill agreement in Section 10, designed to prevent actions that could force JDA to make premature public announcements. By removing this restriction, the changed text creates a loophole where RHI can now indirectly pressure JDA to disclose information, effectively circumventing the standstill's intent as outlined in the contradicted text. The change enables RHI to request actions that, if taken by JDA, would violate the standstill, thus creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, this Agreement may be enforced by any Person with which the Recipient enters into a transaction. Each Party irrevocably and unconditionally submits to the jurisdiction of the federal and state courts located in the State of Delaware, New Castle County, for the purpose of any action, suit or other proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement, and agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in any such court, and further agree that service of process, summons, notice or document by U.S. registered mail to its address set forth in this Agreement will be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement. Each Party hereby also irrevocably and unconditionally (i) waives any objection to the laying of venue of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement in any such federal and state courts, and (ii) waives and agrees not to plead or claim in any such court that such action, suit or proceeding brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party irrevocably and unconditionally submits to the jurisdiction of the federal and state courts located in the State of Delaware, New Castle County, for the purpose of any action, suit or other proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement, and agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in any such court, and further agree that service of process, summons, notice or document by U.S. registered mail to its address set forth in this Agreement will be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement. Each Party hereby also irrevocably and unconditionally (i) waives any objection to the laying of venue of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement in any such federal and state courts, and (ii) waives and agrees not to plead or claim in any such court that such action, suit or proceeding brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the first sentence "Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, this Agreement may be enforced by any Person with which the Recipient enters into a transaction." creates uncertainty about who can enforce the agreement. Section 16 states remedies are available to the party against whom a breach is committed, but section 17 specifies the governing law and jurisdiction. By removing the first sentence, there's now ambiguity. Without it, the contract now seems to imply that enforcement is limited to the parties of the agreement when it comes to jurisdiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Governing Law . This Agreement is for the benefit of each Party and its successors (including any purchaser of such Party) and shall be governed by and construed in 6 accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware applicable to agreements made and to be performed entirely within such state. Each Party irrevocably and unconditionally submits to the jurisdiction of the federal and state courts located in the State of Delaware, New Castle County, for the purpose of any action, suit or other proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement, and agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in any such court, and further agree that service of process, summons, notice or document by U.S. registered mail to its address set forth in this Agreement will be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement. Each Party hereby also irrevocably and unconditionally (i) waives any objection to the laying of venue of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement in any such federal and state courts, and (ii) waives and agrees not to plead or claim in any such court that such action, suit or proceeding brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that 'this Agreement may be enforced by any Person with which the Recipient enters into a transaction.' The changed text omits this sentence. The Contradicted Text includes the same clause regarding jurisdiction as the Changed Text. The explanation states that by removing the sentence, there is now ambiguity regarding who can enforce the agreement, implying that enforcement is limited to the parties of the agreement, creating a contradiction with the understanding of who the agreement benefits and who can enforce it. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction,' where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections, specifically regarding who can benefit from and enforce the agreement.
